name: Demucs (Music Source Separation)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Meta Research; GPU recommended

performance.notes: ~0.5-2x real-time on GPU; slower on CPU; processes full songs in seconds to minutes

agent_readiness.notes: Meta's state-of-the-art music source separation model. Separates audio into stems: vocals, drums, bass, other (guitar, piano, etc.). 4-stem (htdemucs) and 6-stem (htdemucs_6s) models. CLI and Python API. Used for karaoke, remixing, music production, and audio ML training data. HTDemucs model achieves best separation quality. Can run on CPU but GPU much faster.

gotchas: Bleed between stems — some vocal in instrumental and vice versa
Quality degrades for unusual instruments or genres
GPU strongly recommended for practical processing speed
best_when: Music remixing and stem extraction for production
Creating vocal/instrumental training data for audio ML
Music analysis requiring isolated instrument tracks
avoid_when: Perfect isolation required (bleeding artifacts always present)
summary: Demucs is Meta's state-of-the-art music source separation model. Separates audio into 4-6 stems (vocals, drums, bass, guitar, piano, other). Used for karaoke, remixing, and audio ML training data. HTDemucs model achieves best quality. Python API and CLI. GPU recommended for practical speed. Some stem bleed is unavoidable.
